dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini wins by default: HN was skeptical about Chrome/Gemini prompt tools, but the pushback was milder than the sharper negativity aimed at Anthropic’s Claude Code Routines and OpenAI’s cyber-access announcement [E2][E1][E3].
winnerExplanation: No tracked provider had a clearly positive day. Google Gemini comes out ahead only on a relative basis: its signal was mildly negative and limited to security/privacy skepticism around Chrome-integrated Gemini prompt tools, while Anthropic drew the strongest criticism of the day and OpenAI also faced a notably skeptical reception.

openai.judgementSnippet: Negative day: the main OpenAI signal was a skeptical reaction to its "trusted access" cyber-defense announcement, with commenters mocking the post as hype and reporting that verification unlocked nothing tangible [E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Clearly negative day: Claude Code Routines prompted distrust around lock-in, feature churn, degraded coding performance, confusing ToS boundaries, and tighter usage limits [E1].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Slightly negative but best relative day: Chrome/Gemini prompt-tooling drew security, permissions, and unwanted-prompt-history concerns, yet the criticism was milder than what Anthropic and OpenAI received [E2].

evidence.summary: Story: 'Claude Code Routines.' Top comments criticized Anthropic for lock-in risk, unclear ToS, recent usage-limit reductions, feature churn, and worsening Claude coding performance.

Story: 'Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome.' Comments raised security and permissions concerns, complained about Gemini prompt insertion/history behavior, and viewed the feature as premature.

Story: 'Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense.' Comments mocked the announcement as buzzword-heavy, suggested it was a guardrail-loosening PR move, and said verification appeared to unlock nothing.
